---
title: "urge surfing practice"
format: html
---

## Urge Surfing Practice

Urge surfing is a mindfulness-based practice for staying present during cravings without acting on them. Instead of fighting the urge or obeying it, you observe it as a changing experience.

This works because urges shift over time. When you stop reinforcing urges with drinking, they lose strength across repeated episodes. The strongest part of an urge is usually brief; what changes outcomes is how you respond during that window!

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/wave.jpg?raw=true)


::: callout
### Instructions

Use this script during a craving. Stay with each step for 30-60 seconds.

1.  Name the urge: "I am noticing a strong urge to drink."
2.  Notice where it shows up in your body.
3.  Breathe slowly and describe sensations without judging.
4.  Remind yourself: "This wave will pass."
5.  Stay with the urge for 2-3 minutes, then choose a safe action.
:::

### Practice Record

| Trigger | Peak intensity (0-10) | Minutes until it eased | Safe action I chose |
|------------------|------------------|------------------|------------------|
|         |                       |                        |                     |
|         |                       |                        |                     |
|         |                       |                        |                     |
